hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.subtitle: Lesson 8 -- Checkpoint: Introduce Yourself & Your Work
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 8 -- Checkpoint: Introduce Yourself & Your Work
lesson.grammar_point: review: present simple, questions and present continuous

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: an official work trip, often to another country
a person who gives expert advice to businesses
a part of a country with its own area
a planned piece of work with a clear goal
a company or person who works with you
sure about yourself when you speak
to show and talk about your work to a group
to tell people your name and your job for the first time
a list of the topics you will discuss in a meeting

reading.rtitle: Flavia Gets Ready for the Mission
paras: Flavia is a consultant at Sebrae in Sao Paulo. Next month, she travels on an international mission, and she wants to introduce herself and her work in English, with confidence. Tonight she practices her one-minute presentation at home. This is a checkpoint: she puts together everything from Block 1.

'My name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae,' she says out loud. 'I work with small businesses and local products in many regions. My team and I visit companies every week, and we prepare projects together.' Then she practices a question for a partner: 'What does your company do?' Finally, she describes the moment: 'Right now, I am getting ready for a mission abroad.'

In Block 1, Flavia learned to introduce herself, describe her colleagues and her team, talk about small businesses, numbers and regions, and her work day. She learned to ask questions with do and does, and to say what is happening now with the present continuous. Tonight she reviews all of it in one short presentation: who she is, what she does, and what is happening today. She prepares her agenda, and she feels ready and confident.
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A2 checkpoint reading about preparing a self-presentation for an international mission

The text explains how to book a hotel room in another country.

gapfill.text: Three tools from Block 1: present simple for routines (she works), questions with do/does (Do you help...?), and present continuous for now (she is presenting).

bank.label: Useful sentences to introduce yourself and your work
items: My name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae.
I work with small businesses and local products in many regions.
My team and I visit companies every week.
What does your company do, and where are you from?
Do you work with small businesses in your country?
It is a pleasure to introduce my work to you.
Right now, I am presenting our projects at the mission.
I feel ready and confident to speak in English.

lf.title: Present simple, a question, or present continuous?

lf.text: Use the present simple for routines (visit, work), do/does to ask questions, and am/is/are + -ing for what is happening now.

items.situation: Introduce yourself and your job in one sentence.
tips: My name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae.
I am Flavia, a consultant at Sebrae in Brazil.

items.situation: Say what you do at work. Use the present simple.
tips: I work with small businesses and local products in many regions.
My team and I visit companies every week.

items.situation: Ask a partner about their company. Use 'does.'
Say what you are doing right now at the mission. Use present continuous.
tips: Right now, I am presenting my project.
I am meeting new partners at the mission.

items: Introduce yourself in three sentences: your name and role, what you do at Sebrae, and one project. Use your notes.
Ask a partner three questions with do, does, and a question word, then answer one question about your own work.
Give your full one-minute presentation with no notes: who you are, what you do, and what is happening now.
The partner asks: 'What are you doing here at the mission?' Answer with the present continuous: 'Right now, I am presenting our projects and meeting new partners.'

answerkey.title: Reveal the model presentation
list: Model intro: 'My name is Flavia. I am a consultant at Sebrae, and I work with small businesses and local products in many regions.'
present simple for your routine: 'My team and I visit companies every week and prepare projects.'
questions for a partner: 'What does your company do?' 'Do you work abroad?'
present continuous for now: 'Right now, I am getting ready for an international mission.'
answerkey.note: who you are (present simple + be), what you do (present simple), a question (do/does), and what is happening now (present continuous).

listenings.text: Hi Flavia, it's Ana from the office. Your international mission starts next Monday. Right now, the team is preparing your presentation and your agenda. Remember your plan: you introduce yourself first, then you talk about our small businesses and projects. Many partners are arriving from other countries, so speak slowly and with confidence. Good luck, and call me if you need any detail!

Good morning, everyone, and welcome to the International Small Business Mission. The event is starting now. On the main stage, a consultant from Brazil is presenting her projects. In room two, partners are meeting new companies, and many visitors are asking questions. Right now, coffee is available near the entrance. Please introduce yourself to a new partner and enjoy the mission!

extra_audio.text: First, Flavia reviews her words to introduce herself and her work. Then she prepares an agenda and a short project to present. Next, she practices her one-minute presentation out loud. On the day of the mission, she introduces herself to the partners. Finally, she answers their questions and presents her projects with confidence.
